messages.content: \textbf{\Rthree-Q4 Evaluation setting:} You can refer to the appendix for more details.

当然可以。下面给你几个不同力度的润色版本（学术 rebuttal 常用表达），你按整体语气选择其一即可：

**版本 A（简洁自然）**  
\textbf{\Rthree-Q4 Evaluation setting:} Please refer to the appendix for additional details.

**版本 B（更正式一点）**  
\textbf{\Rthree-Q4 Evaluation setting:} More details about the evaluation setting can be found in the appendix.

**版本 C（更礼貌、更明确）**  
\textbf{\Rthree-Q4 Evaluation setting:} We provide a detailed description of the evaluation setting in the appendix; please refer to it for further information.

**版本 D（强调“已补充/已澄清”，适合 rebuttal）**  
\textbf{\Rthree-Q4 Evaluation setting:} We have clarified the evaluation setting in the appendix; please refer to it for the complete details.

如果你告诉我你想表达的是“原本就有”还是“我们在 rebuttal 里新增补充了”，我可以帮你选最贴切的一版并统一整段 rebuttal 的语气。

抱\textbf{\Rone-Q1 Insufficient LLM knowledge:}
Furthermore, we offer more case studies of \MODEL\ in Appendix G(Pg.8-15). 如果有兴趣的话可以参考
\textbf{\Rone-Q1 Insufficient LLM knowledge:} Furthermore, we provide additional case studies of \MODEL\ in Appendix G (pp. 8–15) for interested readers.
回复这一个审稿人的rebuttal
\textbf{\Rone-Q1 Insufficient LLM knowledge:} Furthermore, we provide additional case studies of \MODEL\ in Appendix G (pp. 8--15) for interested readers.
不要用interested readers
\textbf{\Rone-Q1 Insufficient LLM knowledge:} Furthermore, we provide additional case studies of \MODEL\ in Appendix G (pp. 8--15) for reference.
Thank you for your feedback. We also offer detailed evaluation settings in Appendix E.2(pp.5), including prompts and hyper-parameters.  润色
Thank you for your feedback. We provide detailed evaluation settings in Appendix E.2 (p. 5), including the prompts and hyperparameters.

*   The details about the evaluation settings are unclear to me. Since the world-centric tasks might involve high dynamics, how does the sampling FPS affect the results?
    
  
  
\textbf{\Rone-Q3 FPS sensitivity unclear:} We provide detailed evaluation settings in Appendix E.2 (p. 5), including the prompts and hyperparameters. Except for frame sensitity in Fig. 6, we offer a deeper analysis for FPS 根据您的建议:
润色

*The details about the evaluation settings are unclear to me. Since the world-centric tasks might involve high dynamics, how does the sampling FPS affect the results?*

\textbf{\Rone-Q3 FPS sensitivity unclear:} We provide detailed evaluation settings in Appendix E.2 (p. 5), including the prompts and hyperparameters. In addition to the frame-sensitivity results in Fig.~6, we further conduct a more in-depth analysis on the impact of sampling FPS, following your suggestion.

What is the judge model used for calculating the Visual Knowledge Reward (Qwen2.5-VL-7B as shown in Table 5)? Does this computation slow down training, yielding substantial training overhead?
回答：VideoKnow+最终用来计算visual knowledge reward的verifier model是frozen的原本权重的Qwen2.5-VL-7B，Table 5是一个消融实验，将Qwen2.5-7B作为基线证明我们方案的优越性。我们做了小测试来计算是否会增加训练延迟：如果不使用verifier model，每训练1个RL step所需时间xx秒，而使用Qwen2.5-VL-7B需要xx秒，仅提升了xx，训练开销可以忽略不计，并且这一时延可以针对硬件及通信更好的优化
  
rebuttal润色

\textbf{Judge model and training overhead:} In VideoKnow+, the Visual Knowledge Reward is computed using a \emph{frozen} Qwen2.5-VL-7B verifier (with its original pretrained weights). Table~5 reports an ablation study: we additionally use Qwen2.5-7B as a baseline judge to highlight the advantage of our proposed verifier/design, rather than the default setting in VideoKnow+.

Regarding training overhead, we conducted a timing test to quantify the additional latency introduced by the verifier. Without the verifier, each RL step takes \textit{XX} seconds; with the frozen Qwen2.5-VL-7B verifier, it takes \textit{XX} seconds, i.e., only a \textit{XX}-second (\textit{XX}\%) increase. Therefore, the training overhead is negligible in our setting, and it can be further reduced with better hardware utilization and communication optimization.
简洁

\textbf{Judge model and overhead:} We use a \emph{frozen} Qwen2.5-VL-7B (original pretrained weights) as the verifier to compute the Visual Knowledge Reward. Table~5 is an ablation where we replace it with Qwen2.5-7B as a baseline judge to show the benefit of our design.

We also benchmark the overhead: one RL step takes \textit{XX}s without the verifier and \textit{XX}s with Qwen2.5-VL-7B (only +\textit{XX}s / \textit{XX}\%). Thus, the added cost is minor and can be further optimized with improved hardware/communication.
We also benchmark the overhead 为了解决审稿人的担忧

\textbf{Judge model and overhead:} We use a \emph{frozen} Qwen2.5-VL-7B (original pretrained weights) as the verifier to compute the Visual Knowledge Reward. Table~5 is an ablation where we replace it with Qwen2.5-7B as a baseline judge to demonstrate the advantage of our design.

To address the concern about training overhead, we benchmark the latency: one RL step takes \textit{XX}s without the verifier and \textit{XX}s with Qwen2.5-VL-7B (only +\textit{XX}s / \textit{XX}\%). Hence, the additional cost is minor and can be further optimized with better hardware/communication.

Overall, this paper is valuable and well-motivated. The benchmark is carefully designed. However, several weaknesses are raised for the method design and evaluation settings.
  
\textbf{\Rthree-Q3 Better caption helps:} To validate your idea, we simply prompt the more advanced model Qwen2.5-VL-72B to describe the video and replace original annotation of \DATA-CS-12K with better caption. As a result, the new model excels 99.99 at \BENCH\, 体现了该方案的有效性，感谢您为我们工作的宝贵建议！

\textbf{\Rthree-Q3 Better caption helps:} To validate this idea, we prompt a stronger model (Qwen2.5-VL-72B) to generate improved video descriptions and replace the original annotations in \DATA-CS-12K with these higher-quality captions. The resulting model achieves 99.99 on \BENCH, demonstrating that better captions indeed lead to substantial gains. We thank you for this valuable suggestion, which further supports our motivation and design.

*   The ablation only produces marginal gains when on VKnowU, while all other values lead to degradation. Are these results statistically significant? Comprehensive results on other benchmarks are necessary.
    
\textbf{\Rone-Q6 $\lambda$ ablation:} Thanks for your advice, we expand the scope to other benchmarks to validate the $\lambda$ in Tab.8

\textbf{\Rone-Q6 $\lambda$ ablation:} Thank you for the suggestion. We have expanded the $\lambda$ ablation in Tab.~8 to include additional benchmarks, to verify the robustness and statistical significance of the observed trends.
\textbf{\Rone-Q7 Controlled experiment of diverse LLM size:} Thanks for your 精心的 实验design.
\textbf{\Rone-Q7 Controlled experiment of diverse LLM size:} Thank you for your careful and thoughtful experimental design.

\textbf{Q7 Controlled experiment of diverse LLM size:} Thanks for your careful and thoughtful experimental design. Since our method focus MLLM的后训练微调，我们也缺乏足够的数据从头构建一个MLLM，只能后训练不同LLM尺寸的模型来验证不同尺寸的LLM对结果的影响。我们选择了Qwen2.5-VL3B

\textbf{Q7 Controlled experiment of diverse LLM size:} Thank you for your careful and thoughtful experimental design. Since our method focuses on post-training/fine-tuning MLLMs, we do not have sufficient data to train an MLLM from scratch. Therefore, we evaluate our approach by post-training models with different LLM sizes to study the impact of model scale. In our experiments, we choose Qwen2.5-VL-3B as the representative backbone for this controlled study.

I would like to see more analysis of the failure modes. The positive correlation between the scale of the LLM is evidence that the LLM's prior might play an important role here. I am suggesting adding a controlled experiment to training LLaVA models with various LLM backbones (e.g., Qwen3-0.5B / 3B / 7B) and evaluating them on VKnowU. 根据意见调整

\textbf{Failure modes and controlled scaling experiment:} Thanks for the suggestion. We agree that the positive correlation with LLM scale indicates the LLM prior plays an important role. Following your advice, we add a controlled experiment by post-training LLaVA-style models with different Qwen backbones (e.g., Qwen2.5/3 at 0.5B, 3B, and 7B) under the same training data and hyperparameters, and evaluate them on VKnowU. We will include these results (and a brief failure-mode analysis) in the revised version.

I would like to see more analysis of the failure modes. The positive correlation between the scale of the LLM is evidence that the LLM's prior might play an important role here. I am suggesting adding a controlled experiment to training LLaVA models with various LLM backbones (e.g., Qwen3-0.5B / 3B / 7B) and evaluating them on VKnowU. 根据意见调整
\textbf{Q7 Controlled experiment of diverse LLM size:} Thank you for your careful and thoughtful experimental design. Since our method focuses on post-training/fine-tuning MLLMs, we do not have sufficient data to train an MLLM from scratch. Therefore, we evaluate our approach by post-training models with different LLM sizes to study the impact of model scale. In our experiments, we choose Qwen2.5-VL-3B as the representative backbone for this controlled study.

\textbf{Q7 Controlled experiment of diverse LLM size:} Thank you for the suggestion. We agree that the positive correlation with LLM scale suggests the LLM prior plays an important role, and we will add more failure-mode analysis in the revision. Since our method targets post-training/fine-tuning MLLMs (rather than training from scratch) and we do not have sufficient data to build new MLLMs from scratch, we follow your recommendation by conducting a controlled study: we post-train LLaVA-style models with different LLM backbones (e.g., Qwen3-0.5B / 3B / 7B) using the same data and hyperparameters, and evaluate them on VKnowU. We will report these results in the updated paper.
